channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: An Intel-TSMC deal could reshape x86 future and enterprise chip supply chains
item.description: Intel is reportedly in advanced discussions with TSMC to form a joint venture that could potentially reshape the future of the x86 platform and the global semiconductor landscape.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia RTX 5050 and 5060 leaks suggest these mobile GPUs could be pepping up budget gaming laptops soon
item.description: Mobile GPUs are leaked by several notebook makers, giving fresh hope for affordable but nippy gaming laptops.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Genetically Engineered Wolves Resemble Extinct Dire Wolves
item.description: Colossal Biosciences reports on genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves, raising questions about the feasibility of reviving lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: National Weather Service no longer translating products for non-English speakers
item.description: The National Weather Service is no longer providing translations of its products after its contract with an artificial intelligence company was allowed to lapse.
item.dc:creator: Isabella O'Malley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Your Samsung Galaxy S25 just got a huge free Gemini upgrade that gives your AI assistant eyes
item.description: Samsung Galaxy S25 users just got a huge AI upgrade that allows Gemini Live to see what's on your screen and what your camera is looking at.
item.dc:creator: john-anthony.disotto@futurenet.com (John-Anthony Disotto), TechRadar

item.title: Max's #1 most-watched movie Y2K isn't the throwback I wanted – here are 3 better films to stream instead
item.description: Underwhelmed by Max's most popular new movie Y2K? Here are three better films to stream on Max instead.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: TikTok deal pulled after Trump tariff announcement
item.description: A finalized TikTok deal was pulled Thursday after President Trump announced massive new tariffs against China, a source familiar with the negotiations told The Hill. Trump was poised to sign an executive order approving a deal that would have seen TikTok’s U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Google Cloud Next ’25: What to expect
item.description: Google Cloud Next ’25, which runs from April 9-11 in Las Vegas ,will showcase the latest advancements and future directions of Google Cloud Platform and cloud computing in general.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nintendo delays Switch 2 US preorders over Trump tariffs
item.description: Nintendo said U.S. preorders of its Switch 2 console will not take place on April 9 as it assesses tariff implications.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took only seconds for the judges on a New York appeals court to realize that the man addressing them from a video screen — a person about to present an argument in a lawsuit — not only had no law degree, but didn't exist at all.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apple, tariffs, and the art of war
item.description: We are still within the sequence of energies described in Newton’s Third Law of Motion, “every action has an equal and opposite reaction.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why Trump's tariffs are so bad for Big Tech
item.description: President Trump’s sweeping new slate of tariffs is poised to strike a blow at the tech industry, as massive import taxes on China and Taiwan disrupt trade flows that are central to tech firms’ business models.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Leadership, trust, and innovation: The keys to AI’s role in modern warfare
item.description: From ensuring the quality of data to fostering a culture of experimentation and adaptability, leaders must actively steer AI’s integration into operations. The post Leadership, trust, and innovation: The keys to AI’s role in modern warfare first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Tariff war throws building of data centers into disarray
item.description: Enterprise IT leaders are facing a double-whammy of uncertainties complicating their data center building decisions: The ever-changing realities of genAI strategies, and the back-and-forth nature of the current tariff wars pushed by the United States. “This is obviously a fluid situation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft urges Office users to upgrade to 365
item.description: Microsoft is urging Office 2016 and 2019 customers to upgrade to Microsoft 365 before support ends Oct. 14, but analysts said viable alternatives are available outside Microsoft’s walled garden.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Hyperlight Wasm points to the future of serverless
item.description: Back when I first wrote about Hyperlight, Microsoft’s minimal hypervisor, I speculated that it had a possible role as a WebAssembly-based alternative to serverless computing tools like Azure Functions, using Kubernetes as a host.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New MLCommons benchmarks to test AI infrastructure performance
item.description: Industry consortium MLCommons has released new versions of its MLPerf Inference benchmarks, offering a closer look at how current-generation data center and edge hardware performs under increasingly demanding AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Potential Nvidia chip shortage looms as Chinese customers rush to beat US sales ban
item.description: The AI chip shortage could become even more dire as Chinese customers are purportedly looking to hoard Nvidia chips ahead of a proposed US sales ban.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta is fined by Turkey after refusing to restrict content on Facebook and Instagram
item.description: Meta said it has been hit with a hefty fine for resisting Turkish government demands to limit content on Facebook and Instagram.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Splunk launches inventory tool to simplify OpenTelemetry monitoring
item.description: Splunk this week announced a new Service Inventory product that uses OpenTelemetry to offer a comprehensive view into service instrumentation, which the observability company says will solve a critical pain point in modern infrastructure monitoring by providing visibility across cloud and Kubernetes
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia’s Blackwell raises the bar with new MLPerf Inference V5.0 results
item.description: Nvidia on Wednesday released a set of MLPerf Inference V5.0 benchmark results for its Blackwell GPU, the successor to Hopper, saying that its GB200 NVL72 system, a rack-scale offering designed for AI reasoning, set a series of performance records.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Why no one is challenging Trump's executive order that keeps TikTok running
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After TikTok was banned in the United States earlier this year, President Donald Trump gave the platform a reprieve, barreling past a law that was passed in Congress and upheld unanimously by the Supreme Court that said the ban was necessary for national security.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4
